title: Assignment Operator =
subtitle: Put a value inside
goal: Learn how the assignment operator stores a value inside a variable.
explanation: The = operator is used to store a value in a variable.
JavaScript first evaluates the expression on the right side.
Then the resulting value is stored in the variable on the left.
The = sign does not mean mathematical equality. It means "store this value".
This is one of the most important operators in JavaScript.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: The = sign is like a conveyor belt that places a ready item into a labeled box.

remember: = stores a value in a variable.
The right side is evaluated first.
Then the value goes into the variable.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Many beginners think = means "equals". In JavaScript it is the assignment operator, not an equality check.

quiz.question: What does the = operator do?
options: Stores a value in a variable
